title: periploca ciliata
intro: Periploca ciliata, also known as Devil's Claw or Climbing Milkwood, is a perennial climber plant native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain, inflammation, and fever, due to its alkaloid compounds such as harpagoside and harpagide. The health benefits of Periploca ciliata include reducing joint pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of anxiety and insomnia, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the roots and stems, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
